mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports multiple expansion, so declines in the policy rate predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income, and reduced risk appetite, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS and RSAFS both improve together, the economy is generating jobs and sales simultaneously, which indicates broad demand strength and should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement is not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if either series alone explains the effect as well as the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals rising industrial output and improving productive capacity utilization, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO uptrends are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and liquidity conditions ease, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-FEDFUNDS spread trigger does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is controlled for alone.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST signals housing demand weakness and a broader slowdown in interest-sensitive spending, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdowns are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or if the conditional average return is not meaningfully below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market contraction that reduces aggregate income expectations and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up combination does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime, weak GDPC1 growth combined with rising UNRATE confirms contraction in output and employment, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if recessionary GDPC1 weakness plus rising UNRATE is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling out-of-sample tests.
